summary: Operators of the "Rubicon" Center destroyed 191 Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs on April 13, 2026, including over 2,000 intercepted via FPV drones, with 75% of intercepts targeting 12 specific models. On April 11, they destroyed 110 targets, including Ukrainian UAVs and personnel, while coordinating with Russian Aerospace Forces to strike UAV launch sites across the Donbas, Belgorod, and Krasny Liman directions. Ukrainian forces used FPV drones to kill two Russian soldiers on motorcycles and a Russian soldier who attempted to fake surrender, while also employing electronic warfare to force FPV drone landings. Russian soldiers in Kostiantynivka used drone detectors to survive an FPV attack, and the company XDOWN unveiled the STUD drone, a 300 km/h, 64 km range FPV drone with a Switchblade-style foldable design.
